title.zh: 卷五十七上 司馬相如傳
title.raw: 卷五十七上 司馬相如傳 Volume 57a: Sima Xiangru

sentences.zh: 卷五十七上 司馬相如傳 第二十七 上

translations.literal: Volume 57a, Biography of Sima Xiangru, Twenty-Seven, Upper.
translations.idiomatic: Volume 57a: Biography of Sima Xiangru, chapter twenty-seven, first part.

sentences.zh: 司馬相如（上）

translations.literal: Sima Xiangru (Part One).
translations.idiomatic: Sima Xiangru, Part One.

sentences.zh: 司馬相如字長卿，蜀郡成都人也。

translations.literal: Sima Xiangru, style name Zhangqing, was a man of Chengdu in the commandery of Shu.
translations.idiomatic: Sima Xiangru, whose courtesy name was Zhangqing, came from Chengdu in Shu commandery.

sentences.zh: 少時好讀書，學擊劍，名犬子。

translations.literal: In his youth he loved to read books, studied swordsmanship, and his given name was Quanzi.
translations.idiomatic: As a boy he loved books, trained in swordsmanship, and bore the childhood name Quanzi (“Pup”).

sentences.zh: 相如既學，慕藺相如之為人也，更名相如。

translations.literal: After Xiangru had completed his studies, he admired the conduct of Lin Xiangru and changed his name to Xiangru.
translations.idiomatic: Once his schooling was done, he so admired the conduct of Lin Xiangru of Zhao that he adopted the name Xiangru for himself.

sentences.zh: 以訾為郎，事孝景帝，為武騎常侍，非其好也。

translations.literal: By wealth qualification he became a gentleman-attendant and served Emperor Xiaojing as a regular mounted guard, which was not what he loved.
translations.idiomatic: He bought his way into appointment as a gentleman-attendant, served Emperor Jing as a regular palace cavalry guard, and found the post uncongenial.

sentences.zh: 會景帝不好辭賦，是時梁孝王來朝，從遊說之士齊人鄒陽、淮陰枚乘、吳嚴忌夫子之徒，相如見而說之，因病免，客遊梁，得與諸侯游士居，數歲，乃著《子虛之賦》。

translations.literal: It happened that Emperor Jing did not favor rhapsodies; at that time King Xiao of Liang came to court, accompanied by persuader-retainers such as Zou Yang of Qi, Mei Sheng of Huaiyin, Master Yan Ji of Wu, and their sort. Xiangru saw them and was delighted with them; on grounds of illness he resigned his office, traveled as a guest to Liang, was able to dwell with the wandering scholars of the feudal lords, and after several years then composed the “Rhapsody on Sir Vacuity.”
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Jing had little taste for fu rhapsody. Just then King Xiao of Liang was at court with a retinue of persuader-scholars—Zou Yang of Qi, Mei Sheng of Huaiyin, Master Yan Ji of Wu, and others. Sima Xiangru took to their company, pleaded illness, left his post, and went to Liang as a client. For several years he lived among the itinerant literati at the feudal courts, and there he wrote his “Sir Vacuity.”

sentences.zh: 會梁孝王□，相如歸，而家貧無以自業。

translations.literal: When King Xiao of Liang [lacuna in the text], Xiangru returned home, but his family was poor and he had no means to support himself.
translations.idiomatic: After King Xiao of Liang died—at a lacuna in the received text—Xiangru went home, only to find his household too poor to make a living.

sentences.zh: 索與臨邛令王吉相善，吉曰：「長卿久宦游，不遂而困，來過我。」

translations.literal: He had always been on good terms with the magistrate of Linqiang, Wang Ji. Ji said, “Zhangqing has long been an official and wandered abroad; he has not succeeded and is in straits; come visit me.”
translations.idiomatic: He was old friends with Wang Ji, magistrate of Linqiang. Wang Ji said, “Zhangqing, you have been in service and on the road for years without getting anywhere. Come stay with me.”

sentences.zh: 於是相如往捨都亭，臨邛令繆為恭敬，日往朝相如。

translations.literal: Thereupon Xiangru went and lodged at the capital relay lodge. The magistrate of Linqiang pretended great deference and daily went to pay court to Xiangru.
translations.idiomatic: Sima Xiangru moved into the county relay inn. The magistrate feigned elaborate respect and called on him every morning.

sentences.zh: 相如初尚見之，後稱病，使從者謝吉，吉愈益謹肅。

translations.literal: At first Xiangru still received him; afterward he claimed illness and had his followers decline Ji; Ji became all the more careful and solemn.
translations.idiomatic: At first he received Wang Ji, then pleaded illness and sent retainers to turn him away—whereupon the magistrate grew only more punctilious and deferential.

sentences.zh: 臨邛多富人，卓王孫僮客八百人，程鄭亦數百人，乃相謂曰：「令有貴客，為具召之。

translations.literal: Linqiang had many rich men. Zhuo Wangsun had eight hundred retainers, and Cheng Zheng also had several hundred persons. They said to each other, “The magistrate has an honored guest; let us prepare a feast and summon him.
translations.idiomatic: Linqiang swarmed with magnates. Zhuo Wangsun kept eight hundred household hands; Cheng Zheng commanded hundreds as well. They agreed: “The magistrate’s guest is a man of rank—we should lay a banquet and invite him.

sentences.zh: 並召令。」

translations.literal: Summon the magistrate as well.”
translations.idiomatic: Ask the magistrate too.”

sentences.zh: 令既至，卓氏客以百數，至日中請司馬長卿，長卿謝病不能臨。

translations.literal: When the magistrate arrived, Zhuo’s guests numbered in the hundreds; at midday they invited Sima Zhangqing; Zhangqing declined illness and could not come.
translations.idiomatic: When Wang Ji appeared, Zhuo’s hall held a hundred guests. At noon they sent for Sima Zhangqing; he pleaded illness and would not attend.

sentences.zh: 臨邛令不敢嘗食，身自迎相如，相如為不得已而強往，一坐盡傾。

translations.literal: The magistrate of Linqiang did not dare taste food; he personally went to welcome Xiangru; Xiangru, having no choice, went with effort; one sitting and all were overwhelmed.
translations.idiomatic: The magistrate would not touch his meal and went in person to escort him. Sima Xiangru yielded at last and came—and with a single appearance he charmed the whole company.

sentences.zh: 酒酣，臨邛令前奏琴曰：「竊聞長卿好之，願以自娛。」

translations.literal: When the wine was warm, the magistrate of Linqiang stepped forward, played the zither, and said, “I have heard that Zhangqing loves this; I wish to amuse myself with it.”
translations.idiomatic: When they were deep in wine, the magistrate stepped forward with a zither and said, “I hear you are fond of this instrument—may I offer you a tune for your pleasure?”

sentences.zh: 相如辭謝，為鼓一再行。

translations.literal: Xiangru declined with thanks, then drummed out one or two pieces.
translations.idiomatic: Sima Xiangru demurred, then played a passage or two.

sentences.zh: 是時，卓王孫有女文君新寡，好音，故相如繆與令相重而以琴心挑之。

translations.literal: At that time Zhuo Wangsun had a daughter Wenjun who was newly widowed and loved music; therefore Xiangru pretended mutual esteem with the magistrate and with the mind of the zither flirted with her.
translations.idiomatic: Zhuo Wangsun’s daughter Wenjun was newly widowed and loved music. Sima Xiangru therefore played up his friendship with the magistrate and, through what his qin implied, courted her.

sentences.zh: 相如時從車騎，雍容閑雅，甚都。

translations.literal: Xiangru at times followed chariots and riders; he was ample, leisurely, and elegant, very refined.
translations.idiomatic: He arrived with chariot and escort, carrying himself with unhurried grace and unmistakable polish.

sentences.zh: 及飲卓氏弄琴，文君竊從戶窺，心說而好之，恐不得當也。

translations.literal: When he drank at the Zhuo’s and played the zither, Wenjun secretly peeped from the door, was pleased in heart and loved him, yet feared she might not match him.
translations.idiomatic: During the feast at the Zhuo house, as he played, Wenjun stole a glance through a crack in the door—her heart leapt, yet she doubted she could ever be worthy of him.

sentences.zh: 既罷，相如乃令侍人重賜文君侍者通殷勤。

translations.literal: When it was finished, Xiangru then ordered his attendants heavily to reward Wenjun’s maid and convey earnest affection.
translations.idiomatic: Afterward he sent his people to tip her maid handsomely and pass on his tender regard.

sentences.zh: 文君夜亡奔相如，相如與馳歸成都。

translations.literal: Wenjun by night fled to run to Xiangru; Xiangru with her galloped back to Chengdu.
translations.idiomatic: That night Wenjun slipped away to join him, and the two raced back to Chengdu.

sentences.zh: 家徒四壁立。

translations.literal: The house had only the four walls standing.
translations.idiomatic: Their house held nothing but bare walls.

sentences.zh: 卓王孫大怒曰：「女不材，我不忍殺，一錢不分也！」

translations.literal: Zhuo Wangsun in great anger said, “The girl lacks talent; I cannot bear to kill her; I will not give one cash!”
translations.idiomatic: Zhuo Wangsun raged, “My daughter has disgraced herself; I will not put her to death, but she shall not have a single coin from me!”

sentences.zh: 人或謂王孫，王孫終不聽。

translations.literal: Someone spoke to Wangsun; Wangsun to the end would not listen.
translations.idiomatic: Neighbors tried to reason with him; he refused to hear them out.

sentences.zh: 文君久之不樂，謂長卿曰：「弟俱如臨邛，比昆弟假□，猶足以為生，何至自苦如此！」

translations.literal: After a long while Wenjun was unhappy and said to Zhangqing, “Younger brother, let us both go to Linqiang; from brothers borrow [lacuna in text], it is still enough to make a living; why reach to bitter hardship like this!”
translations.idiomatic: After some time Wenjun grew miserable and said, “Husband, let us return to Linqiang. Even if we only borrow from kinsmen on credit, we can still earn a living—anything is better than starving in pride like this.”

sentences.zh: 相如與俱之臨邛，盡賣車騎，買酒捨，乃令文君當盧。

translations.literal: Xiangru with her went to Linqiang, sold off all chariots and riders, bought a wine-shop, and ordered Wenjun to tend the ale counter.
translations.idiomatic: They went back to Linqiang, sold their carriage and horses, opened a tavern, and put Wenjun behind the counter.

sentences.zh: 相如身自著犢鼻褌，與庸保雜作，滌器於市中。

translations.literal: Xiangru himself put on calf-nose breech-clout and mixed with hirelings and inn-boys, washing vessels in the market.
translations.idiomatic: Sima Xiangru himself donned leather breeches like a laborer, worked alongside potmen, and scrubbed cups in the marketplace.

sentences.zh: 卓王孫恥之，為杜門不出。

translations.literal: Zhuo Wangsun was shamed by it and for closing the gate did not go out.
translations.idiomatic: Zhuo Wangsun found the spectacle mortifying and shut his gates, refusing to show his face.

sentences.zh: 昆弟諸公更謂王孫曰：「有一男兩女，所不足者非財也。

translations.literal: Brothers and elders repeatedly said to Wangsun, “You have one son and two daughters; what you lack is not wealth.
translations.idiomatic: His brothers and kinsmen kept urging him: “You have a son and two daughters; you are not short of money.

sentences.zh: 今文君既失身於司馬長卿，長卿故倦游，雖貧，其人材足依也。

translations.literal: Now Wenjun has already lost her person to Sima Zhangqing; Zhangqing by nature is weary of wandering; although poor, his person’s talents are enough to rely on.
translations.idiomatic: Wenjun has given herself to Sima Zhangqing. He may be poor, but he is a man whose talents have tired of mere roaming—she can rely on him.

sentences.zh: 且又令客，奈何相辱如此！」

translations.literal: Moreover he is again the magistrate’s guest; why humiliate him so!”
translations.idiomatic: Besides, he is the magistrate’s guest. Why disgrace him—and yourselves—like this?”

sentences.zh: 卓王孫不得已，分與文君僮百人，錢百萬，及其嫁時衣被財物。

translations.literal: Zhuo Wangsun having no choice, divided and gave Wenjun a hundred retainers, a million cash, and her dowry garments, bedding, and goods.
translations.idiomatic: Zhuo Wangsun yielded at last: a hundred servants, a million cash, and her wedding trousseau went with her.

sentences.zh: 文君乃與相如歸成都，買田宅，為富人。

translations.literal: Wenjun then with Xiangru returned to Chengdu, bought fields and houses, and became rich people.
translations.idiomatic: She and Sima Xiangru returned to Chengdu, bought land and manor, and settled among the wealthy.

sentences.zh: 居久之，蜀人楊得意為狗監，侍上。

translations.literal: After dwelling a long time, a Shu man Yang Deyi was dog-keeper and attended the emperor.
translations.idiomatic: Some time later a fellow townsman, Yang Deyi, served as imperial kennel-master at court.

sentences.zh: 上讀《子虛賦》而善之，曰：「朕獨不得與此人同時哉！」

translations.literal: The emperor read the “Sir Vacuity Rhapsody” and praised it, saying, “How is it that I alone cannot be of the same age as this person!”
translations.idiomatic: The emperor read the “Sir Vacuity” and exclaimed, “Why could I not have lived in the same era as the man who wrote this?”

sentences.zh: 得意曰：「臣邑人司馬相如自言為此賦。」

translations.literal: Deyi said, “My townsman Sima Xiangru himself says he composed this rhapsody.”
translations.idiomatic: Yang Deyi replied, “My countryman Sima Xiangru claims authorship of that piece.”

sentences.zh: 上驚，乃召問相如。

translations.literal: The emperor was startled and then summoned and questioned Xiangru.
translations.idiomatic: Startled, the emperor summoned Sima Xiangru for an audience.

sentences.zh: 相如曰：「有是。

translations.literal: Xiangru said, “It is so.
translations.idiomatic: Sima Xiangru admitted it was his work.

sentences.zh: 然此乃諸侯之事，未足觀，請為天子遊獵之賦。」

translations.literal: Yet this is the affair of feudal lords; it is not enough to view; I request to compose a rhapsody on the Son of Heaven’s hunting.”
translations.idiomatic: “But that piece,” he went on, “only treats the hunts of the feudal lords—it is hardly fit for your majesty. Allow me to offer a fu on the Son of Heaven’s shikar.”

sentences.zh: 上令尚書給筆札，相如以「子虛」，虛言也，為楚稱；

translations.literal: The emperor ordered the Secretary to supply brush and slips; Xiangru took “Sir Vacuity” as vacuous words, speaking for Chu;
translations.idiomatic: The emperor ordered brushes and bamboo slips from the Secretariat. Sima Xiangru explained that “Sir Vacuity” stood for empty boasting on Chu’s behalf;

sentences.zh: 「烏有先生」者，烏有此事也，為齊難；

translations.literal: “Mr. Nonexistence” is that this affair does not exist, speaking for Qi’s rebuttal;
translations.idiomatic: that “Mr. Nonexistence” meant “there is no such thing,” voicing Qi’s counter;

sentences.zh: 「亡是公」者，亡是人也，欲明天子之義。

translations.literal: “Lord No-such-person” is that this person does not exist, intending to clarify the righteousness of the Son of Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: and that “Lord No-such-person” signified no such man at all—figures meant to expound the Son of Heaven’s true standard.

sentences.zh: 故虛借此三人為辭，以推天子諸侯之苑囿。

translations.literal: Therefore he vacuously borrowed these three persons as wording, in order to extend the parks and preserves of Son of Heaven and feudal lords.
translations.idiomatic: He used these three allegorical speakers to unfold the hunting grounds of emperor and kings alike.

sentences.zh: 其卒章歸之於節儉，因以風諫。

translations.literal: Its final chapter returns to moderation and thrift, thereby using indirect remonstrance.
translations.idiomatic: The closing movement turns to restraint and frugality, turning the whole piece into a veiled admonition.

sentences.zh: 奏之天子，天子大說。

translations.literal: He presented it to the Son of Heaven; the Son of Heaven was greatly pleased.
translations.idiomatic: He presented it to the throne, and the emperor was delighted.

sentences.zh: 其辭曰：

translations.literal: Its wording says:
translations.idiomatic: The text runs as follows:

sentences.zh: 楚使子虛使於齊，齊王悉發車騎與使者出田。

translations.literal: Chu sent Sir Vacuity as envoy to Qi; the King of Qi fully deployed chariots and riders and with the envoy went out to hunt.
translations.idiomatic: Chu dispatched Sir Vacuity on a mission to Qi. The king of Qi mustered every chariot and horseman and took his guest out for the chase.

sentences.zh: 田罷，子虛過奼烏有先生，亡是公存焉。

translations.literal: When the hunt ended, Sir Vacuity passed [character defective: 奼] Mr. Nonexistence, and Lord No-such-person was present there.
translations.idiomatic: When the hunt broke up, Sir Vacuity called on Mr. Nonexistence—Lord No-such-person was there too. (The received text is damaged at one character.)

sentences.zh: 坐定，烏有先生問曰：「今日田樂乎？」

translations.literal: When they were seated, Mr. Nonexistence asked, “Was today’s hunt enjoyable?”
translations.idiomatic: Once they were seated, Mr. Nonexistence asked whether the day’s sport had pleased him.

sentences.zh: 子虛曰：「樂。」

translations.literal: Sir Vacuity said, “Enjoyable.”
translations.idiomatic: “It was,” said Sir Vacuity.

sentences.zh: 「獲多乎？」

translations.literal: “Was the catch abundant?”
translations.idiomatic: “Did you take much game?”

translations.literal: He said, “Little.”
translations.idiomatic: “Very little.”

sentences.zh: 「然則何樂？」

translations.literal: “In that case what was enjoyable?”
translations.idiomatic: “Then what pleased you?”

sentences.zh: 對曰：「僕樂王之欲誇僕以車騎之眾，而僕對以雲夢之事也。」

translations.literal: He replied, “I am pleased that the king wished to boast to me of the multitude of his chariots and riders, and I answered with the affair of Cloud Dream.”
translations.idiomatic: Sir Vacuity answered, “I enjoyed the king’s urge to dazzle me with his host of chariots and horsemen—because it let me answer with the tale of Cloud Dream Marsh.”

sentences.zh: 曰：「可得聞乎？」

translations.literal: He said, “May I hear it?”
translations.idiomatic: “May we hear that tale?”

sentences.zh: 子虛曰：「可。

translations.literal: Sir Vacuity said, “Yes.
translations.idiomatic: “Gladly,” said Sir Vacuity.

sentences.zh: 王駕車千乘，選徒萬騎，田於海濱，列卒滿澤，罘罔彌山。

translations.literal: The king drove a thousand chariots, selected ten thousand riders, hunted on the seashore, arrayed soldiers filling the marshes, and seine-nets stretched across the mountains.
translations.idiomatic: “The king fielded a thousand chariots and chose ten thousand riders, hunted along the coast, packed the marshes with beaters, and strung seine-nets ridge to ridge.”

sentences.zh: 掩菟轔鹿，射麋格麟，鶩於鹽浦，割鮮染輪。

translations.literal: They covered hares, crushed deer, shot elk, struck unicorn-like beasts, struck down at the salt flats, cut fresh meat and smeared the wheel hubs with blood.
translations.idiomatic: “They netted hares, rode down deer, shot elk, brought down fabulous quarry, worked the salt flats, and carved fresh kill to daub the chariot wheels with blood.”

sentences.zh: 射中獲多，矜而自功，顧謂僕曰：『楚亦有平原廣澤遊獵之地饒樂若此者乎？

translations.literal: The shots hit and the catch was abundant; he was proud and credited himself; looking back he said to me, “Does Chu also have level plains and broad marshes and roaming-hunt places rich and joyful like this?
translations.idiomatic: “His aim was true and the bag grew fat; flushed with pride he turned to me and asked whether Chu owned any hunting ground as vast and merry as his own.

sentences.zh: 楚王之獵孰與寡人？』

translations.literal: How does the King of Chu’s hunt compare with me, the unworthy one?”
translations.idiomatic: How, he demanded, did the king of Chu’s sport measure against his?”

sentences.zh: 仆下車對曰：『臣，楚國之鄙人也，幸得宿衛十有餘年，時從出遊，游於後園，覽於有無，然猶未能遍睹也。

translations.literal: I dismounted the chariot and replied, “Your servant is a vulgar man of Chu; fortunately I have been night guard more than ten years; at times following on outings, I roamed the rear garden and surveyed what there was and was not; yet still I have not been able to see it all.
translations.idiomatic: “I stepped down and answered, ‘I am a humble Chu subject who has stood night watch at court for a dozen years. I have sometimes followed the hunt in the rear park and glimpsed something of what lies there, yet I have never seen it all.’”

sentences.zh: 又烏足以言其外澤乎？』

translations.literal: How is it enough to speak of its outer marshes?”
translations.idiomatic: How then could I speak of the great marsh beyond?’”

sentences.zh: 齊王曰：『雖然，略以子之所聞見言之。』

translations.literal: The King of Qi said, “Although it is so, briefly, according to what you have heard and seen, speak.”
translations.idiomatic: “The king of Qi said, ‘Even so, tell us in outline what you have seen and heard.’”

sentences.zh: 「僕對曰：『唯唯。

translations.literal: “I replied, ‘Yes, yes.
translations.idiomatic: “I answered, ‘Very well.’”

sentences.zh: 臣聞楚有七澤，嘗見其一，未睹其餘也。

translations.literal: I have heard that Chu has seven marshes; I have seen one of them; I have not beheld the rest.
translations.idiomatic: They say Chu holds seven great marshes; I have visited only one and know nothing of the others.

sentences.zh: 臣之所見，蓋特其小小者耳，名曰雲夢。

translations.literal: What I have seen is probably only its smallest small one; its name is Cloud Dream.
translations.idiomatic: The tract I saw must be the smallest of the lot, and it is still called Cloud Dream.

sentences.zh: 雲夢者，方九百里，其中有山焉。

translations.literal: Cloud Dream is nine hundred li square; within it there are mountains.
translations.idiomatic: Cloud Dream spans nine hundred li, and mountains rise inside it.

sentences.zh: 其山則盤紆岪郁，隆崇律崒；

translations.literal: As for its mountains, they coil and writhe, steep and piled;
translations.idiomatic: Those peaks twist in long folds and pile into dizzy heights;

sentences.zh: 岑崟參差，日月蔽虧；

translations.literal: summits jagged and uneven, sun and moon hidden and eclipsed;
translations.idiomatic: their crests jag so wildly that sun and moon seem swallowed;

sentences.zh: 交錯糾紛，上干青雲；

translations.literal: intertwined and tangled, they ascend into the blue clouds;
translations.idiomatic: they tangle upward until they scrape the sky;

sentences.zh: 罷池陂□，下屬江河。

translations.literal: slopes undulate [lacuna]; below they connect to the Yangtze and Han rivers.
translations.idiomatic: their lower slopes roll down in long undulations to meet the great rivers. (One character is missing in the text.)

sentences.zh: 其土則丹青赭堊，雌黃白附，錫碧金銀，眾色炫耀，照爛龍鱗。

translations.literal: As for its soil, cinnabar and azurite, ochre and white clay, orpiment and white arsenic, tin, jadeite, gold, and silver—many colors dazzle, shining like dragon scales.
translations.idiomatic: The earth yields cinnabar and malachite, red ochre and white clay, orpiment and arsenic pigments, tin, green jade, gold, and silver—colors so bright they flash like dragon scales.

sentences.zh: 其石則赤玉玫瑰，琳鈱昆吾，□□玄厲，□石武夫。

translations.literal: As for its stones, red jade, rose-quartz, fine jade, Kunwu stone, [lacuna] black flint, [lacuna] whetstone.
translations.idiomatic: The stones include carnelian, rose quartz, Kunwu jade, and other hard gems—several names are partly lost in the received text.

sentences.zh: 其東則有蕙圃，衡蘭芷若，穹窮昌蒲，江離蘪蕪，諸柘巴且。

translations.literal: On its east are orchid gardens, thoroughwort, eupatorium, angelica, skandix, iris, river mallow, and miscanthus, sugarcane and ginger.
translations.idiomatic: To the east lie beds of orchids, thoroughwort, angelica, skandix, sweet-flag, river mallow, and tall reeds, with cane and ginger.

sentences.zh: 其南則有平原廣澤，登降□靡，案衍壇曼，緣以大江，限以巫山。

translations.literal: On its south are level plains and broad marshes, ascending and descending [lacuna], level stretches and spreading expanses, bordered by the great Yangtze, bounded by Mount Wu.
translations.idiomatic: To the south stretch level plains and wide meres, rising and falling in gentle swells, hemmed by the Yangtze and closed in by Mount Wu.

sentences.zh: 其高燥則生葴析苞荔，薜莎青薠。

translations.literal: On its high dry ground grow sedge, oak-leaf thorn, pepper, and water-caltrop; smilax, sedge, and green sedge.
translations.idiomatic: The high ground bears sedge, thorn-oak, pepper trees, and water caltrop, mingled with smilax and river rushes.

sentences.zh: 其埤濕則生藏莨蒹葭，東{艸牆}雕胡，蓮藉觚盧，奄閭軒於。

translations.literal: On its low wet ground grow wild rice, reed, eastern wall, wild rice stem, lotus root, arrowhead, water-chestnut, and basella.
translations.idiomatic: The wet bottoms grow wild rice, reeds, bindweed, water bamboo, lotus, arrowhead, water chestnut, and basella vine.

sentences.zh: 眾物居之，不可勝圖。

translations.literal: The multitude of things dwelling there cannot be fully depicted.
translations.idiomatic: The plants and creatures that live there are beyond counting or description.

sentences.zh: 其西則有湧泉清池，激水推移，外發夫容{艸陵}華，內隱巨石白沙。

translations.literal: On its west are gushing springs and clear pools, surging water pushed along; outwardly lotus and water-lily bloom, inwardly huge rocks and white sand are hidden.
translations.idiomatic: To the west, jets of clear water feed restless pools where lotus and water-lilies break the surface while boulders and white sand lie beneath.

sentences.zh: 其中則有神龜蛟□，毒冒鱉黿。

translations.literal: Within them are divine turtles, horned dragons [lacuna], soft-shelled turtles and great yuans.
translations.idiomatic: In those depths swim spirit turtles, horned dragons, and giant soft-shells—though one creature name is damaged in the manuscript.

sentences.zh: 其北則有陰林巨樹，□楠豫章，桂椒木蘭，檗離硃楊，□梨□栗，橘柚芬芳。

translations.literal: On its north are shady forests and huge trees, [lacuna] nan and Parashorea, cinnamon and pepper, magnolia, yellow birch and red poplar, [lacuna] pear [lacuna] chestnut, orange and pomelo fragrant.
translations.idiomatic: To the north stand shadowy groves of great trees—nanmu, camphor, cassia, pepper, magnolia, birch, and poplar, with fruiting pear and chestnut, orange and pomelo perfuming the air. (Several tree names are incomplete in the text.)

sentences.zh: 其上則有宛雛孔鸞，騰遠射干。

translations.literal: Above them are pheasants, peacocks, long-jumping lynxes, and ye gan.
translations.idiomatic: Above fly pheasants, peacocks, and long-tailed mountain pheasants.

sentences.zh: 其下則有白虎玄豹，□蜒貙□。

translations.literal: Below them are white tigers and black panthers, [lacuna] yans and [lacuna] chu.
translations.idiomatic: Below roam white tigers, black leopards, and other fierce cats whose names the text only partly preserves.

sentences.zh: 於是乎乃使剸諸之倫，手格此獸。

translations.literal: Thereupon he then sent men such as the Zhuans, with their hands to grapple these beasts.
translations.idiomatic: The king then sent professional hunters like the Zhuans to bring the beasts down bare-handed.

sentences.zh: 楚王乃駕馴駁之駟，乘雕玉之輿，靡魚須之橈旃，曳明月之珠旗，建干將之雄戟，左鳥號之雕弓，右夏服之勁箭；

translations.literal: The King of Chu then drove matched teams of broken horses, rode a chariot carved with jade, spread fish-whisker tassels on the yak-tail banners, trailed bright-moon pearl pennons, raised the male ji halberd of Ganjiang, hung the carved bow Bird-cry on the left, and strong arrows of Xia quiver on the right;
translations.idiomatic: The king of Chu yoked teams of trained horses, mounted a jade-inlaid carriage, flew banners fringed with yak hair and pearl streamers, brandished the great Ganjiang halberd, slung the famed “Bird-cry” bow on the left and filled the right with Xia-made shafts.

sentences.zh: 陽子驂乘，□阿為御；

translations.literal: Yangzi acted as side-rider; [lacuna] A was charioteer;
translations.idiomatic: Yangzi rode escort while a named charioteer held the reins—the driver’s name is partly lost.

sentences.zh: 案節未舒，即陵狡獸，蹴蛩蛩，轔距虛，軼野馬，□惠□□余；

translations.literal: Checking the pace not yet relaxed, then they overleaped crafty beasts, trampled qiong-qiong, crushed ju-xu, passed wild horses, [lacunas];
translations.idiomatic: Before the horses had quite slackened pace they were leaping crafty quarry, trampling fabulous beasts, outrunning wild horses—several beast names are defective in the text.

sentences.zh: 乘遺風，射游騏，倏胂倩□，雷動焱至，星流電擊，弓不虛發，中必決眥，洞胸達掖，絕乎心系，獲若雨獸，□草蔽地。

translations.literal: They mounted swift wind, shot roaming qi; in a flash [lacunas]; thunder moved, flame arrived, stars streamed, lightning struck; the bow did not loose empty, every hit tore the canthus, pierced chest through armpit, severed the heart’s ties; the catch fell like rain beasts, [lacuna] grass covered the ground.
translations.idiomatic: They rode the gale, shot at full gallop; the hunt roared like thunder and flashed like lightning—no arrow flew idle, every hit dropped the quarry in its tracks until carcasses littered the grass like rain. (The received line is damaged in several places.)

sentences.zh: 於是楚王乃弭節徘徊，翱翔容與，覽乎陰林，觀壯士之暴怒，與猛獸之恐懼，徼□受詘，殫睹眾物之變態。

translations.literal: Thereupon the King of Chu then checked the pace, lingered wandering, soared leisurely, surveyed the shady forest, watched the warriors’ violent rage and the fierce beasts’ terror, exhausted and submitted the frightened, utterly saw the multitude of things’ transformations.
translations.idiomatic: Then the king checked his team, wheeled slowly, and idled through the shadowed woods, savoring the fury of his hunters and the terror of the beasts until every quarry had yielded and the spectacle was spent.

sentences.zh: 於是鄭女曼姬，被阿錫，揄□縞，雜纖羅，垂霧□，襞積褰縐，郁橈溪谷；

translations.literal: Thereupon Zheng girls and lovely concubines, wearing fine silk, drawing [lacuna] white gauze, mixed thin gauze, hung mist-[lacuna], pleated skirts lifted in folds, curved like ravines and valleys;
translations.idiomatic: Then came the palace girls of Zheng in thin silks and layered gauze, skirts fluttering like mist over winding valleys—though the fabric names are partly corrupted.

sentences.zh: □，揚□戌削，蜚□垂□；

translations.literal: [lacuna], raised [lacuna] trim and tight, flying [lacuna] hanging [lacuna];
translations.idiomatic: Their sleeves flared, their sashes flew—several phrases are illegible in the received text.

sentences.zh: 扶輿猗靡，翕呷萃蔡，下摩蘭蕙，上拂羽蓋；

translations.literal: Supporting the carriage, leaning and swaying, huffing and puffing in rustling silk, below rubbing orchids and thoroughwort, above brushing feathered canopy;
translations.idiomatic: They swayed with the chariot, their silks hissing as they brushed the orchids below and stroked the feathered awning above.

sentences.zh: 錯翡翠之葳蕤，繆繞玉綏；

translations.literal: They set jadeite in luxuriant layers, twined jade pendants;
translations.idiomatic: Jade ornaments flashed at waist and wrist, knotted sashes swinging with each sway.

sentences.zh: 眇眇忽忽，若神之彷彿。

translations.literal: Dim and fleeting, as if spirits’ shadows.
translations.idiomatic: They seemed less mortal women than drifting spirits.

sentences.zh: 於是乃群相與獠於蕙圃，□姍勃□，上金堤，□翡翠，射□鸃，微矰出，□繳施，弋白鵠，連駕鵝，雙倉□，揚旌□，張翠帷，建羽蓋。

translations.literal: Thereupon they all together drove game in the orchid garden, [lacuna] on the golden dike, [lacuna] jadeite, shot [lacuna], fine bolts flew, [lacuna] cords were set, they shot white swans, linked wild geese, double [lacuna], raised banners [lacuna], spread kingfisher curtains, erected feathered canopies.
translations.idiomatic: Together they beat the orchid park for game along golden embankments, loosing arrows at swans and geese, raising colored banners and kingfisher drapes—many words in this catalogue are damaged.

sentences.zh: 罔毒冒，釣紫貝，摐金鼓，吹鳴籟，榜人歌，聲流喝，水蟲駭，波鴻沸，湧泉起，奔揚會，□石相擊，琅琅□蓋□，若雷霆之聲，聞乎數百里外。

translations.literal: They netted soft-shelled turtles, hooked purple cowries, struck golden drums, blew sounding pipes; boatmen sang, voices flowing and calling, water creatures were startled, wave geese boiled, gushing springs rose, rushing waves met, [lacuna] stones struck each other, [lacuna] sounds, like thunder’s noise, heard several hundred li outside.
translations.idiomatic: They netted turtles, hooked cowries, beat drums and blew flutes until boat-songs and crashing surf blended into a roar like thunder heard a hundred leagues away. (The line is partly defective.)

sentences.zh: 「『將息獠者，擊靈鼓，起烽燧，車案行，騎就隊，纚乎淫淫，般乎裔裔。

translations.literal: “‘When they were about to rest the drivers, they struck the spirit drum, raised beacon fires, chariots moved in file, riders formed ranks, flowing long, moving like waves.
translations.idiomatic: “When the beaters were recalled, the spirit drum rolled, signal fires flared, chariots fell into line and riders into ranks, streaming on in endless file.”

sentences.zh: 於是楚王乃登陽雲之台，泊乎無為，淡乎自持，勺藥之和具而後御之。

translations.literal: Thereupon the King of Chu then ascended the Yangyun terrace, anchored in non-action, bland in self-control, peony harmony prepared and afterward partook of it.
translations.idiomatic: The king of Chu then climbed the Yangyun Terrace, let his mind rest in stillness, and in quiet restraint waited until the spiced banquet was laid before he tasted it.

sentences.zh: 不若大王終日馳騁，曾不下輿，□割輪焠，自以為娛。

translations.literal: It is not like the great king’s all-day galloping, never once descending from the chariot, [lacuna] cutting wheels and roasting fresh meat, taking that as amusement.
translations.idiomatic: “That is nothing like you, my lord, who spend the whole day at the gallop without leaving your chariot, slice hot meat against the wheel hubs, and call that pleasure.” (One verb is missing in the text.)

sentences.zh: 臣竊觀之，齊殆不如。』

translations.literal: Your servant secretly observed it; Qi probably does not compare.’
translations.idiomatic: “From what I could see, Qi could scarcely compete.”’

sentences.zh: 於是王無以應僕也。」

translations.literal: Thereupon the king had nothing with which to answer your servant.”
translations.idiomatic: “At that the king of Qi had no answer left for me.”

sentences.zh: 烏有先生曰：「是何言之過也！

translations.literal: Mr. Nonexistence said, “How excessive is this way of speaking!
translations.idiomatic: Mr. Nonexistence broke in: “That is carrying the argument much too far!

sentences.zh: 足下不遠千里，來況齊國，王悉境內之士，備車騎之眾，與使者出田，乃欲戮力致獲，以娛左右也，何名為誇哉！

translations.literal: You, sir, did not shrink from a thousand li, came to condescend to visit Qi; the king mustered all the gentlemen within the borders, prepared the multitude of chariots and riders, and with the envoy went out to hunt, intending to join strength to obtain game, in order to amuse those at his side—what name is this for boasting!
translations.idiomatic: You traveled a great distance to honor Qi with a visit. The king called up every able man in the realm, marshaled chariots and horsemen, and took you hunting so that together you might bag game and give his courtiers a pleasant day out—how is that “boasting”?

sentences.zh: 問楚地之有無者，願聞大國之風烈，先生之餘論也。

translations.literal: As for him who asked whether Chu’s land has such things, he wished to hear the great state’s imposing airs and the gentleman’s surplus discourse.
translations.idiomatic: When he asked what there was in Chu, he wanted to hear something of Qi’s renown and your own reflections—not a lecture on Chu’s parks.

sentences.zh: 今足下不稱楚王之德厚，而盛推雲夢以為驕，奢言淫樂而顯侈靡，竊為足下不取也。

translations.literal: Now you, sir, do not praise the Chu king’s generous virtue, but greatly push Cloud Dream to make pride, extravagant words and dissolute pleasures and display extravagance—I privately think this is not what you, sir, should take.
translations.idiomatic: Yet you never praise the king of Chu’s magnanimity; instead you inflate Cloud Dream into a boast, wallow in sensual excess, and parade every kind of luxury. I cannot think that becomes you.

sentences.zh: 必若所言，固非楚國之美也。

translations.literal: If it must be as you say, it is indeed not Chu’s beauty.
translations.idiomatic: If things were really as you painted them, they would be no credit to Chu at all.

sentences.zh: 有而言之，是章君之惡也；

translations.literal: If there is and you speak it, this displays the ruler’s evil;
translations.idiomatic: If they exist and you retail them, you expose your ruler’s faults;

sentences.zh: 無而言之，是害足下之信也。

translations.literal: If there is not and you speak it, this harms your own, sir, trustworthiness.
translations.idiomatic: if they do not and you still retail them, you ruin your own credibility.

sentences.zh: 章君惡，傷私義，二者無一可，而先生行之，必且輕於齊而累於楚矣。

translations.literal: Displaying the ruler’s evil, injuring private righteousness—of the two not one is acceptable, yet the gentleman has done it; he will surely be held light by Qi and burdened by Chu.
translations.idiomatic: Either way you wound both ruler and guest. To behave so is to earn contempt in Qi and bring trouble down on Chu.

sentences.zh: 且齊東□巨海，南有琅邪，觀乎成山，射乎之罘，浮勃澥，游孟諸，邪與肅慎為鄰，右以湯谷為界。

translations.literal: Moreover Qi to the east [lacuna] the vast sea; to the south has Langya; they gaze on Mount Cheng, shoot at Zhifu, float on the Bo Gulf, roam Mengzhu; slantingly with Sushen are neighbors; to the right takes Tang Valley as boundary.
translations.idiomatic: Consider Qi itself: the eastern sea bounds it—one character is missing in the text—Langya lies to the south, with hunts at Mount Cheng and Zhifu, fleets on the Bohai Gulf, and excursions in the Mengzhu marsh; Sushen lies athwart its frontier and the coast runs toward the place of the rising sun.

sentences.zh: 秋田乎青丘，仿□乎海外，吞若雲夢者八九，其於匈中曾不蒂芥。

translations.literal: Autumn hunt at Qingqiu, roam [lacuna] beyond the seas; swallowing such as Cloud Dream in eight or nine, in the breast they are not even mustard-seed.
translations.idiomatic: The king hunts the Qingqiu in autumn and ranges beyond the sea—yet tracts the size of eight or nine Cloud Dreams would still sit on his chest no heavier than a speck of dust.

sentences.zh: 若乃□倘瑰瑋，異方殊類，珍怪鳥獸，萬端鱗崒，充仞其中者，不可勝記，禹不能名，□不能計。

translations.literal: As for [lacuna] wandering rare and grand, exotic regions and special kinds, precious strange birds and beasts, ten thousand tips like fish scales, filling and overflowing within them—cannot be fully recorded; Yu could not name them, [lacuna] could not count them.
translations.idiomatic: Rare creatures and curiosities from every quarter teem there beyond count or naming—several words are lost in the manuscript, including one verb paired with Yu.

sentences.zh: 然在諸侯之位，不敢言游戲之樂，苑囿之大；

translations.literal: Yet in the position of feudal lords, he dare not speak of the pleasures of roaming games, the greatness of parks and preserves;
translations.idiomatic: Yet a feudal lord may not brag of his sport or the size of his hunting parks.

sentences.zh: 先生又見客，是以王辭不復，何為無以應哉！」

translations.literal: Moreover the gentleman is again a guest; therefore the king declined words and did not reply—why call it having nothing to answer!”
translations.idiomatic: You are a guest of honor; the king held his tongue out of courtesy—not because he had nothing to say!”

sentences.zh: 亡是公聽然而笑曰：「楚則失矣，而齊亦未為得也。

translations.literal: Lord No-such-person listened and laughed, saying, “Chu then erred, yet Qi also has not obtained correctness.
translations.idiomatic: Lord No-such-person laughed aloud and said, “Chu was in the wrong—but Qi fares no better.

sentences.zh: 夫使諸侯納貢者，非為財幣，所以述職也；

translations.literal: As for making the feudal lords present tribute, it is not for wealth and goods, but the means by which they report their duties;
translations.idiomatic: Tribute from the lords is not about collecting treasure; it is how they render account of their stewardship.

sentences.zh: 封疆畫界者，非為守禦，所以禁淫也。

translations.literal: As for drawing borders and marking boundaries, it is not for guarding and defending, but the means by which excess is forbidden.
translations.idiomatic: Fixed frontiers are not meant chiefly for defense; they are meant to curb excess and ambition.

sentences.zh: 今齊列為東蕃，而外私肅慎，捐國□限，越海而田，其於義固未可也。

translations.literal: Now Qi is arrayed as the eastern hedge, yet outwardly treats privately with Sushen, abandons the state’s [lacuna] limits, crosses the sea to hunt; as to righteousness, it is indeed not permissible.
translations.idiomatic: Qi ranks as an eastern bulwark of the realm, yet it cultivates private ties with Sushen, casts off its proper bounds, and hunts beyond the sea—conduct that the rites cannot justify. (One character is missing in the text.)

sentences.zh: 且二君之論，不務明君臣之義，正諸侯之禮，徒事爭於遊戲之樂，苑囿之大，欲以奢侈相勝，荒淫相越，此不可以揚名發譽，而適足以貶君自損也。

translations.literal: Moreover the two gentlemen’s discourse does not exert itself to clarify the righteousness of ruler and subject, to correct the ritual of feudal lords, but vainly strives in the pleasures of roaming games, the greatness of parks and preserves, wishing by extravagance to overcome each other, by dissolute excess to surpass each other—this cannot raise name and spread praise, but is exactly sufficient to demean the rulers and harm themselves.
translations.idiomatic: Both of you ignore the bond between sovereign and subject and the etiquette owed by a vassal state; you only vie over whose playground is larger and whose hunt is more lavish. That wins no glory—it only shames your masters and yourselves.

sentences.zh: 「且夫齊、楚之事又烏足道乎！

translations.literal: “Moreover the affairs of Qi and Chu—how are they worth speaking of!”
translations.idiomatic: “As for Qi and Chu—what is there even worth saying!”

sentences.zh: 君未睹夫巨麗也，獨不聞天子之上林乎？

translations.literal: You, sir, have not beheld the great magnificence; have you alone not heard of the Son of Heaven’s Shanglin Park?”
translations.idiomatic: You have never seen true magnificence. Surely you have heard of the Son of Heaven’s Shanglin preserve?”

sentences.zh: 左蒼梧，右西極，丹水更其南，紫淵徑其北。

translations.literal: Zangwu to the left, the western extreme to the right; the Vermilion River runs past its south, the Purple Abyss crosses its north.
translations.idiomatic: It runs toward Cangwu in the east and toward the western wastes; the Vermilion River washes its southern flank and the Purple Pool its northern.

sentences.zh: 終始霸、產，出入涇、渭，□、鎬、潦、□，紆余委蛇，經營其內。

translations.literal: Beginning and ending with Ba and Chan, entering and exiting Jing and Wei, [lacuna], Hao, Lao, [lacuna], winding long, coiling within it.
translations.idiomatic: The Ba and Chan rivers thread through it from end to end; the Jing and Wei pour in and out, joined by the Feng, Hao, Lao, and other streams—several river names are damaged in the text—that wind and coil across the park.

sentences.zh: 蕩蕩乎八川分流，相背異態，東西南北，馳騖往來，出乎椒丘之闕，行乎州淤之浦，逕乎桂林之中，過乎泱莽之野，汩乎混流，順阿而下，赴隘□之口，觸穹石，激堆□，沸乎暴怒，洶湧彭湃，滭弗宓汩，逼側泌瀄，橫流逆折，轉騰潎洌，滂濞沆溉，穹隆雲橈，宛水單膠□，逾波趨乂，蒞蒞下瀨，批巖沖擁，奔揚滯沛，臨坻注壑，□霣隊，沈沈隱隱，砰磅訇□蓋，□淈□，□鼎沸，馳波跳沫，汩□漂疾，悠遠長懷。

translations.literal: Vastly the eight streams divide flow, turn backs with different forms, east west south north, gallop and rush back and forth, emerge from Pepper Hill’s gap, move on islet shallows, pass through Cassia Forest’s midst, cross the boundless wilds, boil in mingled currents, follow the bend downward, hasten to the narrow [lacuna] mouth, strike vaulting rocks, smash heaped [lacuna], bubble in violent rage, surge and swell, boil and seethe, press close and rush, cross flow reverse twist, turn and leap sparkling, pound and flood, arch high cloud-curved, wind water glue [lacuna], cross waves hasten currents, arrive arrive descend rapids, strike cliffs dash banks, run and splash dam-full, overlook islets pour into gullies, [lacuna] fall in ranks, deep deep hidden hidden, pound crash thunder [lacuna], [lacuna] welling, [lacuna] cauldron-boil, galloping waves leaping foam, swift [lacuna] drifting fast, far far long longing.
translations.idiomatic: Eight great rivers split and surge through the park, each with its own temper: they burst from defiles, braid across sandbars, thread the cassia groves, flood the open moors, then boil together, drop through narrows—one place name is lost—hammer boulders and shoals, rear in white rage, crash and cross-cut until the whole basin seethes like a cauldron. The passage piles on binomes for roaring water; many graphs are missing or corrupt in the received Hanshu.

sentences.zh: 寂□無聲，肆乎永歸。

translations.literal: Silent [lacuna] without sound, freely then forever return.
translations.idiomatic: Then all at once the torrent subsides into a deep hush, and the waters glide home to rest. (One character is missing.)

sentences.zh: 然後灝□潢漾，安翔徐□，□乎□，東注大湖，衍溢陂池。

translations.literal: Then [lacuna] vast and flowing, peacefully soaring slowly [lacuna], [lacuna] then [lacuna], eastward pour into the great lake, overflow and fill slope pools.
translations.idiomatic: Spreading wide, the currents ease and slide eastward into the great lake, spilling over into every marsh and pond along the way.

sentences.zh: 於是蛟龍赤螭，□恆□瞢漸離，□禺禺□鰨，健鰭掉尾，振鱗奮翼，潛處乎深巖。

translations.literal: Thereupon horned dragons and red chi, [lacuna] gradually leave, [lacuna] fish [lacuna] sole, strong fins wag tails, shake scales spread wings, hide in deep crags.
translations.idiomatic: Horned dragons and lesser river dragons thrash their fins and fan their scales in the depths—several fish names are defective in the manuscript.

sentences.zh: 魚鱉歡聲，萬物眾伙。

translations.literal: Fish and turtles sound joy; the myriad things are many in kind.
translations.idiomatic: Fish and turtles sport on the surface; countless creatures flourish in the flood.

sentences.zh: 明月珠子，的皪江靡，蜀石黃□，水玉磊砢，磷磷爛爛，采色澔汗，叢積乎其中。

translations.literal: Bright moon pearls, sparkling along the river’s edge, Shu stones yellow [lacuna], water jade piled high, gleaming bright, colors vast and brilliant, cluster-accumulated within them.
translations.idiomatic: Lustrous pearls line the banks; Shu jade, yellow crystal, and heaped “water jade” flash so many hues that the riverbed seems paved with gems.

sentences.zh: 工□鷫鵠鴇，鴽鵝屬玉，交精旋目，煩鶩庸渠，箴疵□盧，群浮乎其上。

translations.literal: Craft [lacuna] siskin swan bustard, ru goose jade duck, cross-eyed turning-eye, vexed duck canal duck, zhen ci [lacuna] ruddy, flock float upon its surface.
translations.idiomatic: Wildfowl of every description—swans, bustards, teals, and rarer species partly lost in the text—raft in dense flocks on the water.

sentences.zh: 浮淫氾濫，隨風澹淡，與波搖蕩，奄薄水□，唼喋菁藻，咀嚼鞭藕。

translations.literal: Floating dissolute overflow, following wind light ripple, sway with the waves, cover near water [lacuna], peck and chatter on green algae, chew lotus root.
translations.idiomatic: They drift with the breeze, bob on the chop, dabble in the shallows, and peck at algae and lotus root.

sentences.zh: 「於是乎崇山矗矗，巃嵷崔巍，深林巨木，嶄巖參差。

translations.literal: “Thereupon towering mountains stand steep, lofty and precipitous, deep forests and huge trees, sheer crags uneven.
translations.idiomatic: Beyond the rivers rise sheer ranges and deep woods, cliffs stepping wildly toward the sky.

sentences.zh: 九□，南山峨峨，巖□錡，{山椎}崛崎，振溪通谷，蹇產溝瀆，呀豁□，阜陵別島，崴□□□，丘陵崛□，隱轔郁□壘，登降施靡，陂池□豸。

translations.literal: Nine [lacuna], South Mountain lofty, crag [lacuna] strange, [mountain radical] jutting steep, shake ravines connect valleys, winding produce ditches, gaping open [lacuna], mound-islands separate islets, [lacuna], hill mounds jutting [lacuna], hidden carriage dense [lacuna] ramparts, ascending descending spreading, slope pools [lacuna].
translations.idiomatic: The Southern March looms among broken peaks and winding gorges; ridges, islets, and hillocks pile one upon another in a maze the damaged text can only partly name.

sentences.zh: 允溶淫鬻，散渙夷陸，亭皋千里，靡不被築。

translations.literal: Even melt overflow, scatter on level land, pavilion marshes a thousand li, none not covered and built.
translations.idiomatic: The floodplain spreads smooth for a thousand li, every terrace and bottomland groomed for the imperial pleasure.

sentences.zh: □以綠蕙，被以江離，糅以蘼蕪，雜以留夷。

translations.literal: [lacuna] with green thoroughwort, cover with river mallow, mix with river mallow, blend with liuyi.
translations.idiomatic: The ground is sewn with thoroughwort, river mallow, and other aromatics—one verb is lost at the line’s opening.

sentences.zh: 布結縷，攢戾莎，揭車衡蘭，稿本射干，茈姜蘘荷，葴持若蓀，鮮支黃礫，蔣□青薠，布□閎澤，延曼太原，離靡廣衍，應風披靡，吐芳揚烈，鬱鬱菲菲，眾香發越，□蠁布寫，晻薆□。

translations.literal: Spread knotweed, gather sharp sedge, lift cart thoroughwort, straw root belamcanda, purple ginger lotus, zhen chi ruo sun, fresh branch yellow gravel, Jiang [lacuna] green sedge, spread [lacuna] vast marsh, extend long plain, separate spread wide extend, respond to wind lean over, spit fragrance raise fierce scent, dense dense fragrant fragrant, many fragrances rise surpass, [lacuna] spread write, dim [lacuna].
translations.idiomatic: Herbs and spices carpet the flats in such profusion—knotweed, belamcanda, ginger, iris, and a host of other plants, some unreadable in the manuscript—that the wind carries a single intoxicating breath across miles of marsh.

sentences.zh: 「於是乎周覽泛觀，縝紛軋芴，芒芒恍忽，視之無端，察之無涯。

translations.literal: “Thereupon round viewing broadly observing, confused pressed swift, vast vast dim dim, look at it without beginning, examine it without shore.
translations.idiomatic: Roam where you will: the view dissolves into haze and dazzle, without verge or limit.

sentences.zh: 日出東沼，入乎西陂。

translations.literal: The sun emerges from the eastern marsh, enters the western slope.
translations.idiomatic: The sun lifts from the eastern pool and sinks behind the western levee.

sentences.zh: 其南則隆冬生長，湧水躍波；

translations.literal: On its south then deep winter things grow, gushing water leaps in waves;
translations.idiomatic: To the south it is so mild that plants grow in the depth of winter and springs boil up in dancing waves;

sentences.zh: 其獸則庸旄貘□，沈牛麝麋，赤首圜題，窮奇象犀。

translations.literal: As for its beasts then yong mao mo [lacuna], sink ox musk elk, red head round forehead, qiongqi elephant rhinoceros.
translations.idiomatic: there you find yak, tapir, musk deer, rhinoceros, elephant, and the fabulous qiongqi—several animal names are incomplete in the text.

sentences.zh: 其北則盛夏含凍裂地，涉冰揭河；

translations.literal: On its north then midsummer contains frozen splitting earth, wade ice lift river;
translations.idiomatic: To the north it stays so cold that in midsummer the ground can split with frost and men can ford the ice;

sentences.zh: 其獸則麒麟角端，□駼橐駝，蛩蛩驒騱，驒騠驢騾。

translations.literal: As for its beasts then qilin horned-end, [lacuna] tuo camel, qiong-qiong tuo-xia, tuo-ti donkey mule.
translations.idiomatic: there graze unicorn, camel, wild ass, mule, and other steppe beasts—one name is lost.

sentences.zh: 「於是乎離宮別館，彌山跨谷，高廊四注，重坐曲閣，華□璧榼，輦道纚屬，步檐周流，長途中宿。

translations.literal: “Thereupon detached palaces and separate lodges, fill mountains span valleys, high corridors pour on four sides, double seats winding pavilions, splendid [lacuna] jade cup, carriage-way continuous, walking eaves circulate all round, long road enough to lodge overnight.
translations.idiomatic: Detached palaces span ridge after ridge: high galleries, tiered belvederes, covered walks that run for a day’s journey without leaving the roofed ways.

sentences.zh: 夷□築堂，累台增成，巖突洞房。

translations.literal: Level [lacuna] build hall, pile terrace add story, crag jutting deep chamber.
translations.idiomatic: Terraces rise in stacked stories, their inner chambers bored deep into the living rock.

sentences.zh: 俯杳眇而無見，仰攀□而捫天，奔星更於閨闥，宛虹拖於□軒。

translations.literal: Looking down dim vast without seeing, looking up climb [lacuna] and touch sky, shooting stars pass through inner doors, winding rainbow trails across [lacuna] window.
translations.idiomatic: Look down and the depth swallows sight; look up and you seem to brush the sky—meteors slip past the chamber doors and rainbows arch through the lattices.

sentences.zh: 青龍蚴□於東箱，像輿婉僤於西清，靈圉燕於閒館，偓□之倫暴於南榮，醴泉湧於清室，通川過於中庭。

translations.literal: Green dragon coils [lacuna] in east wing, elephant carriage gentle in west clear, spirit guards feast in quiet lodge, wo quan’s sort expose in south eave, sweet spring gushes in pure chamber, flowing river passes through middle courtyard.
translations.idiomatic: Bronze dragons coil along the east wing, jade-mounted chariots stand in the western gallery, guardian spirits are cast in the side halls, and a sweet spring bubbles up through the central court.

sentences.zh: 磐石□崖，嶔巖倚傾，嵯峨{山集}嶪，刻削崢嶸，玫瑰碧琳，珊瑚叢生，鈱玉旁唐，玢豳文磷，赤瑕駁犖，雜□其間，□采琬琰，和氏出焉。

translations.literal: Massive rock [lacuna] cliff, towering steep leaning, towering [mountain] steep, carved sharp jutting, rose quartz jadeite, coral cluster-grow, fine jade broad spread, patterned phosphor, red flaw mottled, mixed [lacuna] among them, [lacuna] colors fine jade, He’s jade emerges there.
translations.idiomatic: Cliffs of raw jade and coral, grain on grain of carnelian and “He’s” famous disk—the stone itself seems to bloom with color.

sentences.zh: 「於是乎盧橘夏孰，黃甘橙楱，楷杷橪柿，亭柰厚樸，□棗楊梅，櫻桃蒲陶，隱夫薁棣，答□離支，羅乎後宮，列乎北園，□丘陵，下平原，揚翠葉，□紫莖，發紅華，垂硃榮，煌煌扈扈，照曜巨野。

translations.literal: “Thereupon dark orange summer ripe, yellow sweet orange sour-orange, loquat olive persimmon, crabapple thick pear, [lacuna] jujube bayberry, cherry grape, yin fu yu plum, da [lacuna] lychee, spread in rear palace, array in north garden, [lacuna] hill mounds, descend level plain, raise emerald leaves, [lacuna] purple stems, send out red flowers, hang vermilion fruit, brilliant brilliant following following, illuminate shine the great wilds.
translations.idiomatic: Orchards fill the rear palace and north park with oranges, loquats, persimmons, cherries, grapes, lychees, and every southern fruit—green leaves, purple stalks, scarlet bloom, and fruit glowing like coals across the hills. (Several tree names are damaged.)

sentences.zh: 沙棠櫟櫧，華楓枰櫨，留落胥邪，仁頻並閭，□檀木蘭，豫章女貞，長千仞，大連抱，誇條直暢，實葉□茂，攢立叢倚，連捲欐，崔錯□，坑稀□，垂條扶疏，落英幡纚，紛溶□蔘，猗□從風，藰蒞卉歙，蓋象金石之聲，管□之聲音。

translations.literal: Sand pear oak zelkova, splendid maple plane catalpa, liuluo xuye, ren pin bing lu, [lacuna] sandalwood magnolia, Parashorea female chaste, long thousand ren, great encircle arms, boast branches straight smooth, fruit leaves [lacuna] lush, cluster stand cluster lean, connect curl [lacuna], peak wrong [lacuna], pit rare [lacuna], hang branches spreading, fall flowers fluttering, dissolve [lacuna], lean [lacuna] follow wind, arrive [lacuna] flower breathe, cover resemble metal stone sounds, pipe [lacuna] sounds.
translations.idiomatic: Forest giants—oaks, zelkovas, camphors, magnolias—stand thicker than a man can span, their limbs interlaced until the wind in the leaves sounds like bells and flutes. Many plant names in this catalogue are corrupt.

sentences.zh: 柴池茈□，旋還乎後宮，雜襲累輯，被山緣谷，循阪下□，視之無端，究之亡窮。

translations.literal: Chai pool zi [lacuna], whirl return in rear palace, mixed pile repeated gather, cover mountains along valleys, follow slopes down [lacuna], look at it without beginning, exhaust it without end.
translations.idiomatic: The groves run in belts round the inner palaces, cloak every slope, and stretch away without visible end.

sentences.zh: 「於是乎玄□素雌，蜼□飛□，蛭蜩□，獑胡□，棲息乎其間。

translations.literal: “Thereupon black [lacuna] white female, long-tailed ape flying [lacuna], zhi tiao [lacuna], chan hu [lacuna], roost and dwell within them.
translations.idiomatic: Black gibbons, white gibbons, long-tailed apes, and other mountain monkeys swing and roost in the canopy—several species names are lost.

sentences.zh: 長嘯哀鳴，翩幡互經，夭蟜枝格，偃蹇杪顛，逾絕梁，騰殊榛，捷垂條，掉希間，牢落陸離，爛溫遠遷。

translations.literal: Long howl sad cry, flutter cross each other, bend twist branch frame, recline lofty treetop, leap broken bridge, leap special thicket, seize hanging branch, swing rare gap, scattered scattered separate separate, blaze warm far move.
translations.idiomatic: They howl, flash from limb to limb, vault broken bridges and thickets, and vanish in a rustle of leaves.

sentences.zh: 「若此者數百千處，娛游往來，宮宿館舍，包廚不徙，後宮不移，百官備具。

translations.literal: “Such as this several hundred thousand places, roaming play coming going, palace lodge overnight, wrapped kitchen does not move, rear palace does not shift, hundred officials all prepared.
translations.idiomatic: Hundreds of such lodges dot the park; the court may roam for months while pantries, harem, and full bureaucracy travel with the throne.

sentences.zh: 「於是乎背秋涉冬，天子校獵。

translations.literal: “Thereupon turning back autumn crossing winter, the Son of Heaven conducts the grand hunt.
translations.idiomatic: When autumn turns to winter, the emperor leads the great battue.

sentences.zh: 乘鏤象，六玉□，拖蜺旌，靡雲旗，前皮軒，後道游；

translations.literal: Ride engraved elephant, six jade [lacuna], trail rainbow banners, spread cloud flags, in front leather screen wagon, behind dao-you;
translations.idiomatic: He mounts an ivory-inlaid chariot drawn by six gleaming bays—one ornament name is missing—rainbow pennons and cloud standards streaming, a leather-screened vanguard and escort chariots to the rear.

sentences.zh: 孫叔奉轡，衛公參乘，扈從橫行，出乎四校之中。

translations.literal: Sun Shu holds reins, Duke of Wei acts side-rider, retinue follows moving crosswise, emerge within the four camps.
translations.idiomatic: Sun Shu takes the reins, the Duke of Wei rides escort, and the host breaks from the four-field cordon in full array.

sentences.zh: 鼓嚴簿，縱獵者，江河為□，泰山為櫓，車騎雷起，殷天動地，先後陸離，離散別追，淫淫裔裔，緣陵流澤，雲布雨施。

translations.literal: Drum strict roster, release hunters; Yangtze and Yellow River serve as [lacuna], Mount Tai as shield; chariots and riders thunder rise, shake heaven move earth, first last scattered separate, separate scatter pursue apart, flowing long moving like waves, follow hills flow marshes, cloud spread rain cast.
translations.idiomatic: The roll call ends, the beaters open, and the hunt spills across a front measured in rivers and mountains—chariots thunder, earth shakes, squadrons wheel like clouds bursting into rain.

sentences.zh: 生貔豹，搏豺狼，手熊羆，足野羊。

translations.literal: Strip pi leopard, strike jackal wolf, hand bear pi, foot wild goat.
translations.idiomatic: Men strip the pelts from leopards, wrestle wolves and bears, and run down wild goats with their bare feet.

sentences.zh: 蒙鶡蘇，褲白虎，被斑文，跨野馬，陵三□之危，下磧歷之坻，逕峻赴險，越壑厲水。

translations.literal: Wear pheasant feather, trousers white tiger, covered spotted pattern, straddle wild horse, ascend three [lacuna] danger, descend gravel stony islet, pass steep rush danger, leap gully ford water.
translations.idiomatic: They wear plumed hoods and tiger-skin chaps, ride bareback, and charge slopes and gravel beds that would stop an ordinary horse.

sentences.zh: 推蜚廉，弄解□，格蝦蛤，鋌猛氏，□要□，射封豕。

translations.literal: Push feilian, play jie [lacuna], strike xia ge, stab Mengshi, [lacuna] yao [lacuna], shoot great boar.
translations.idiomatic: They spear wind-spirits, gore fabulous beasts, and bring down giant boars—several monster names are defective.

sentences.zh: 箭不苟害，解□陷腦；

translations.literal: Arrows do not carelessly harm, split [lacuna] pierce brain;
translations.idiomatic: No arrow flies without purpose; each strike splits skull or spine.

sentences.zh: 弓不虛發，應聲而倒。

translations.literal: Bow does not loose empty, respond to sound and fall.
translations.idiomatic: No bowstring hums without answer; game drops at the report.

sentences.zh: 「於是乘輿弭節徘徊，皋翔往來，睨部曲之進退，覽將帥之變態。

translations.literal: “Thereupon the carriage checks pace and lingers wandering, high soar coming going, glance at the companies’ advance retreat, observe the generals’ transformations.
translations.idiomatic: The imperial chariot checks, wheels, and surveys the columns as commanders shift their dispositions.

sentences.zh: 然後侵淫促節，倏□遠去，流離輕禽，蹴履狡獸，□惠白鹿，捷狡菟。

translations.literal: Then gradually hasten pace, suddenly [lacuna] far go, scatter drift light birds, tread crafty beasts, [lacuna] hui white deer, seize crafty rabbit.
translations.idiomatic: Then the pace quickens: birds scatter before the cars, clever beasts are trampled underhoof, white deer and swift hares are run down—one verb is missing.

sentences.zh: 軼赤電，遺光耀，追怪物，出宇宙，彎蕃弱，滿白羽，射游梟，櫟蜚遽。

translations.literal: Pass red lightning, leave light glory, pursue strange creatures, emerge universe, bend Fanruo, fill white feather, shoot wandering owl, strike flying ju.
translations.idiomatic: They outrace storm-flash, bend the Fanruo bow, loose white-feathered shafts, and strike down owls and other ill-omened birds mid-flight.

sentences.zh: 擇肉而後發，先中而命處，弦矢分，□僕。

translations.literal: Choose flesh then loose, first hit then name the place; bowstring arrow part, [lacuna] fall.
translations.idiomatic: They pick their quarry before they shoot and call the spot before the arrow lands—one final verb is damaged.

sentences.zh: 「然後揚節而上浮，陵驚風，歷駭焱，乘虛亡，與神俱，藺玄鶴，亂昆雞，遒孔鸞，促□鸃，指翳鳥，捎鳳凰，捷鵷雛，□焦明。

translations.literal: “Then raise whip and ascend float, mount startling wind, pass frightening flame, ride empty nothing, together with spirits, pursue dark crane, confuse Kun cock, hasten peacock, urge [lacuna] yi, point yi bird, flick phoenix, seize yuan-chu, [lacuna] jiao ming.
translations.idiomatic: The hunt then takes wing: chariots seem to ride the wind and flame after every rare bird from dark crane to phoenix—several bird names are incomplete in the text.

sentences.zh: 「道盡塗殫，回車而還。

translations.literal: “Way exhausted path ended, turn chariot and return.
translations.idiomatic: When trails and game alike are spent, the chariots wheel for home.

sentences.zh: 消□乎襄羊，降集乎北□，率乎直指，□乎反鄉，蹶石關，歷封巒，過□鵲，望露寒，下堂□，息宜春，西馳宣曲，濯□牛首，登龍台，掩細柳，觀士大夫之勤略，鈞獵者之所得獲。

translations.literal: Wandering far in roaming [lacuna], descend gather in north [lacuna], lead straight to, [lacuna] return home, pass Stone Gate, pass Fengluan, pass [lacuna] magpie, gaze Dew-cold, descend Hall [lacuna], rest Yichun, west gallop Xuanqu, wash [lacuna] Ox-head, ascend Dragon Terrace, cover Willow, observe officers and gentlemen’s diligent strategy, weigh hunters’ catches.
translations.idiomatic: The procession drifts through the park’s gates and lodges—Shimen, Fengluan, and the rest, many names partly lost—while the emperor reviews what his men have taken.

sentences.zh: 徒車之所□轢，騎之所蹂若，人之所蹈藉，與其窮極倦，□驚憚讋伏，不被創刃而死者，它它藉藉，填坑滿谷，掩平彌澤。

translations.literal: Where foot chariots have [lacuna] crushed, where riders have trampled, where men have trod, together with their exhausted frightened [lacuna] alarm submit crouch, not receiving blade wounds yet dead, other other piled piled, fill gullies full valleys, cover level fill marshes.
translations.idiomatic: Beasts crushed under wheel and hoof, stunned without a blade’s touch, lie heaped until gorges and flats are carpeted with carcasses.

sentences.zh: 「於是乎遊戲懈怠，置酒乎顥天之台，張樂乎膠葛之□，撞千石之鐘，立萬石之□，建翠北之旗，樹靈□之鼓，奏陶唐氏之舞，聽葛天氏之歌，千人倡，萬人和，山陵為之震動，川谷為之蕩波。

translations.literal: “Thereupon roaming play slack and idle, set wine on Haotian terrace, spread music in Jiaoge’s [lacuna], strike thousand-stone bell, erect ten-thousand-stone [lacuna], raise kingfisher north flag, plant spirit [lacuna] drum, perform Tao Tang’s dance, listen to Getian’s song, a thousand lead, ten thousand harmonize, hill mounds for it shake move, river valleys for it surge waves.
translations.idiomatic: Then, weary of sport, they feast on the Terrace of Vast Heaven, strike bells of a thousand stone-weights, raise kingfisher banners and spirit drums, and stage the dances of antiquity until hills tremble and valleys answer like surf.

sentences.zh: 巴、俞、宋、蔡，淮南《干遮》，文成顛歌，族居遞奏，金鼓迭起，鏗鎗□，洞心駭耳。

translations.literal: Ba, Yu, Song, Cai, Huainan “Gan zhe,” Wencheng’s mad song, clan dwell relay perform, metal drums rise in turn, clang [lacuna], penetrate heart shake ear.
translations.idiomatic: Troupes from Ba, Yu, Song, Cai, and Huainan follow one another; bronze drums and wild songs strike the heart and ring in the ears.

sentences.zh: 荊、吳、鄭、衛之聲，《韶》、《□》、《武》、《象》之樂，陰淫案衍之音，鄢、郢繽紛，《激楚》、《結風》，俳優侏儒，狄□之倡，所以娛耳目樂心意者，麗靡爛漫於前，靡曼美色於後。

translations.literal: Jing, Wu, Zheng, Wei sounds, “Shao,” “[lacuna],” “Wu,” “Xiang” music, yin dissolute level-stretch tones, Yan Ying confused, “Excite Chu,” “Knot Wind,” jesters dwarfs, Di [lacuna] singers, those by which to amuse ear and eye gladden heart intent, beautiful dissolute spread before, soft slow beautiful colors behind.
translations.idiomatic: The licentious airs of Zheng and Wei alternate with the classical Shao and Wu suites, acrobats and dwarfs, and sensual dances—everything that dazzles eye and ear and works on the heart.

sentences.zh: 「若夫青琴、虙妃之徒，絕殊離俗，妖冶閒都，靚莊刻飾，便□綽約，柔橈□，嫵媚纖弱，曳獨繭之□□，眇閻易以恤削，便姍□屑，與世殊服，芬芳漚郁，酷烈淑郁，皓齒粲爛，宜笑的皪，長眉連娟，微睇綿藐，色授魂予，心愉於側。

translations.literal: “As for Qingqin Fufei’s sort, cut off special leave vulgar, alluring fine refined, quiet dress carved adorn, nimble [lacuna] graceful, soft yielding [lacuna], charming delicate slender weak, trail single cocoon’s [lacuna], dim easy in thin pare, pian xian [lacuna], with world special dress, fragrant dense rich, strong scent pure rich, white teeth bright, fit laugh sparkle, long eyebrows connected graceful, slight glance continuous dim, color give soul grant, heart pleased at side.
translations.idiomatic: Ladies like the goddess Fufei move among the guests in gauze so fine it seems spun from a single cocoon—glances that steal the soul and laughter that flashes like pearls. Several adjectives are corrupt in the source.

sentences.zh: 「於是酒中樂酣，天子芒然而思，似若有亡，曰：『嗟乎，此大奢侈！

translations.literal: “Thereupon in the midst of wine music warm, the Son of Heaven dimly ponders, as if having something lost, saying, “Alas, this is great extravagance!
translations.idiomatic: Deep in wine and music the emperor’s mood turns grave, as if something had been lost, and he murmurs, “This is too much—far too much.

sentences.zh: 朕以覽聽餘閒，無事棄日，順天道以殺伐，時休息於此，恐後世靡麗，遂往而不返，非所以為繼嗣創業垂統也。』

translations.literal: I by surveying listening spare leisure, without affairs abandon days, follow heaven’s way thereby to kill and attack, at times rest here, fear later generations dissolute beautiful, follow gone and not return, this is not the means by which to bequeath heir establish enterprise hang continuity.”
translations.idiomatic: I used my free hours to hunt in accord with the seasons, never dreaming that posterity might ape this splendor, drift into excess, and never find their way back—that is no foundation for an heir or a dynasty.”

sentences.zh: 於是乎乃解酒罷獵，而命有司曰：『地可墾闢，悉為農郊，以贍氓隸，隤牆填塹，使山澤之民得至焉。

translations.literal: Thereupon then unbind wine cease hunt, and ordered the responsible officials, saying, “Land that can be opened for tillage, all make agricultural outskirts, thereby to enrich commoners and slaves; tear down walls fill moats, enable mountain marsh people to reach there.
translations.idiomatic: He ended the revel and ordered his ministers to open every acre that could be plowed, throw down the park walls and fill the ditches, and let hill folk and marsh dwellers come in to farm.

sentences.zh: 實陂池而勿禁，虛官館而勿仞。

translations.literal: Fill slope pools yet do not forbid, empty official lodges yet do not occupy.
translations.idiomatic: Let the people fish the ponds without ban and lodge in the empty guesthouses.

sentences.zh: 發倉廩以救貧窮，補不足，恤鰥寡，存孤獨。

translations.literal: Open granaries thereby to save the poor and destitute, replenish insufficiency, pity widowers and widows, preserve orphans and alone.
translations.idiomatic: Release grain for the hungry, aid the short of supplies, comfort widows and widowers, and shelter the orphaned and alone.

sentences.zh: 出德號，省刑罰，改制度，易服色，革正朔，與天下為始。』」

translations.literal: Issue virtuous edicts, reduce punishments, change institutions, alter dress colors, reform first month of year, with all under heaven make a beginning.”’”
translations.idiomatic: Proclaim amnesty, lighten penalties, reform the calendar, change court dress—give the empire a new beginning.”’”

sentences.zh: 「於是歷吉日以齋戒，襲朝服，乘當駕，建華旗，鳴玉鸞，游於六藝之囿，馳騖乎仁義之塗，覽觀《春秋》之林，射《□首》，兼《騶虞》，弋玄鶴，舞干戚，戴雲□，□群雅，悲《伐檀》，樂樂胥，修容乎《禮》園，翱翔乎《書》圃，述《易》道，放怪獸，登明堂，坐清廟，恣群臣，奏得失，四海之內，靡不受獲。

translations.literal: “Thereupon choosing auspicious days thereby to fast and purify, put on court dress, ride proper chariot, raise splendid flags, sound jade luan-bells, roam in the Six Arts’ park, gallop on the path of benevolence and righteousness, survey the “Spring and Autumn” forest, shoot “[lacuna] head,” also “Zouyu,” shoot dark crane, dance shield-axe, wear cloud [lacuna], [lacuna] group Ya, mourn “Felling sandalwood,” rejoice “Le le xu,” cultivate appearance in the “Rites” garden, soar in the “Documents” grove, set forth the “Changes’” way, release strange beasts, ascend Bright Hall, sit in Clear Temple, indulge the hundred officials, present gains and losses; within the four seas none do not receive bounty.
translations.idiomatic: On a chosen fast day he dons full court robes, sounds his jade bells, and “hunts” instead through the garden of the Six Classics—archery to the moral odes, dances with shield and axe, discourses on the Rites, Documents, and Changes, frees the menagerie’s fabulous beasts, holds court in the Bright Hall, and lets every officer speak plain truth until the realm shares the benefit.

sentences.zh: 於欺之時，天下大說，鄉風而聽，隨流而化，□然興道而遷義，刑錯而不用，德隆於三皇，功羨於五帝。

translations.literal: At this time, all under heaven greatly pleased, turned to the wind and listened, followed the flow and transformed, [lacuna] then rose in the Way and moved to righteousness, punishments set aside yet not used, virtue elevated beyond the Three August Ones, merit surpassing the Five Thearchs.
translations.idiomatic: At that the world rejoiced, habits turned with his edict, and virtue rose until punishments fell idle—an achievement said to eclipse even the Five Thearchs. (One character is missing.)

sentences.zh: 若此，故獵乃可喜也。」

translations.literal: If like this, therefore hunting is then something to be pleased in.”
translations.idiomatic: When the hunt ends in such deeds, it is truly a thing to celebrate.”

sentences.zh: 「若夫終日馳騁，勞神苦形，罷車馬之用，□士卒之精，費府庫之財，而無德厚之恩，務在獨樂，不顧眾庶，忘國家之政，貪雉菟之獲，則仁者不繇也。

translations.literal: “As for all day galloping, toiling spirit bittering body, exhausting chariot-horse use, [lacuna] soldier elite, wasting treasury wealth, yet without generous virtue’s grace, striving only in solitary pleasure, not caring for the multitude, forgetting state affairs, greedy for pheasant rabbit’s catch—then the humane man will not follow it.
translations.idiomatic: But to race all day until men and horses are spent, drain the treasury, enrich no one, and care only for bagging hares—that a humane ruler will not do.

sentences.zh: 從此觀之，齊、楚之事，豈不哀哉！

translations.literal: From this viewing it, Qi and Chu’s affairs—how are they not pitiful!
translations.idiomatic: Seen in that light, the quarrel of Qi and Chu is pathetic indeed.

sentences.zh: 地方不過千里，而囿居九百，是草木不得墾闢，而民無所食也。

translations.literal: The land’s area does not exceed a thousand li, yet the park occupies nine hundred—this is grass and trees cannot be opened for tillage, and the people have nothing to eat.
translations.idiomatic: Their territories are less than a thousand li square, yet nine-tenths is park—no room to plow, nothing for the people to live on.

sentences.zh: 夫以諸侯之細，而樂萬乘之所侈，僕恐百姓被其尤也。」

translations.literal: As for using the feudal lords’ smallness, yet rejoicing in what the myriad-chariot ruler indulges in extravagantly, your servant fears the hundred surnames will suffer their blame.”
translations.idiomatic: Petty lords who mimic an emperor’s excess invite disaster on their commoners—and that, sir, is what I fear for your people.”

sentences.zh: 於是二子愀然改容，超若自失，逡巡避席，曰：「鄙人固陋，不知忌諱，乃今日見教，謹受命矣。」

translations.literal: Thereupon the two gentlemen somberly changed countenance, super as if self-lost, hesitating withdrew from mat, saying, “We vulgar men are indeed rustic, do not know taboo; only today receive instruction; respectfully accept the command.”
translations.idiomatic: The two visitors blanched, rose unsteadily from their cushions, and said, “We are coarse men who spoke out of turn. We thank you for the lesson and shall take it to heart.”

sentences.zh: 賦奏，天子以為郎。

translations.literal: When the rhapsody was presented, the Son of Heaven made him a gentleman.
translations.idiomatic: When the fu was laid before the throne, the emperor appointed Sima Xiangru a gentleman.

sentences.zh: 亡是公言上林廣大，山谷水泉萬物，及子虛言雲夢所有甚眾，侈靡多過其實，且非義理所止，故刪取其要，歸正道而論之。

translations.literal: Lord No-such-person’s speech on Shanglin’s vastness, mountains valleys water springs myriad things, and Sir Vacuity’s speech on Cloud Dream’s possessing very much, extravagant dissolute much exceeding the reality, and moreover not what righteousness and principle rest in—therefore delete and take their essentials, return to the correct Way and discuss them.
translations.idiomatic: Ban Gu’s note: Lord No-such-person’s description of Shanglin and Sir Vacuity’s catalogue of Cloud Dream swell beyond fact and stray from moral purpose; the historian has therefore trimmed them to essentials and set the discourse back on the proper path.
